domain: Curling sweeping (Olympic sport): brooms create kinetic-to-heat conversion producing thin transient water film on ice; reduces friction; selective one-side broom controls curl; melt is reversible; energy spent only where needed only when needed.
specific_mechanism: Transient local energy injection produces transient local low-friction state; surface state modified in advance of moving object then reverts.
llm_application: CURLING-SWEEP transient runtime-repair low-precision compute: at inference, monitor per-step smoothness signal s_t (output logit entropy or top-1 probability). When s_t below low-entropy threshold (high confidence 'easy' decode), sweep NEXT k tokens (k=5) to fp8 precision in FFN layers. When s_t rises (high uncertainty), revert to fp16. Sweep window is transient + reversible per token. Differs from (a) Progressive Mixed-Precision Decoding (phase-scheduled not signal-triggered), (b) Speculative decoding (draft+verify paradigm not precision-switch), (c) Static mixed-precision quantization (no runtime switch), (d) Phase-aware mixed-precision by combining (i) PER-STEP SMOOTHNESS SIGNAL + (ii) DYNAMIC LOOK-AHEAD WINDOW lower precision + (iii) PER-TOKEN REVERSIBLE switch.
novelty_claim: Progressive Mixed-Precision Decoding varies precision by phase. ResQ uses low-rank residuals. Speculative decoding uses draft model. CURLING-SWEEP recombines (a) ENTROPY-TRIGGERED switch + (b) LOOK-AHEAD WINDOW + (c) PER-TOKEN REVERSIBLE. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
